target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Round 2 review fixes (2026-05-24)

Addressed all three reviewer findings on the previous head `d81505c539`: P1 ΓÇö bridged tools now honor the resolved sandbox context (caller-side `resolveSandboxContext` in `attempt.ts` plus `sandbox`/`spawnWorkspaceDir` forwarded through `tool-bridge.ts`); P2 ΓÇö `@github/copilot-sdk` is now an optional `peerDependency` with an on-demand install (~260 MB) wired into the wizard via `src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts` and a typed dynamic loader in `extensions/copilot/src/sdk-loader.ts`; and the unrelated `plugin: meeting-notes` labeler block is restored in `.github/labeler.yml`. Validated locally with `tsgo:core` + `tsgo:extensions` clean, `check:import-cycles` 0, `extensions/copilot` 19 files / 424 tests passing (was 18/409 before the fixes), 14 new tests in `src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.test.ts`, 4 in `src/agents/copilot-routing.test.ts`, and a live gateway chat-completion smoke against `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` returning the literal expected reply with full prompt-token usage (22,469 tokens including the bootstrap-files context).

## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording — N/A (CLI / HTTP).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: Redesign Overview as an operator-first OpenClaw dashboard
target.body:
## Summary
- Rework the connected Overview page into an operator-first dashboard instead of a setup/status landing page.
- Keep first-run Gateway Access and Snapshot cards intact for disconnected installs, then move them below daily operating signals once the Control UI is connected.
- Use existing gateway, session, cron, auth, provider/quota, connector, skill, and log data. This PR intentionally avoids adding new Gateway RPCs.

## Major functional improvements
- Makes the Overview useful at a glance: the first screen now answers what needs attention, what just ran, whether model/provider usage looks healthy, and whether cron/connectors/skills/logs look normal.
- Reduces dashboard scrolling: Attention and Recent Sessions stop growing without bound, and the usage/quota surface now uses a compact metric layout instead of a tall card.
- Respects OpenClaw's multi-model posture: the dashboard presents provider/quota health from the data available in the install, without implying OpenClaw is tied to one LLM provider.
- Shows clearer provider-limit detail when providers expose different kinds of limits: time buckets, model-tier buckets, token budgets, credit balances, and monthly quotas use provider-reported labels instead of a generic "quota" tile.
- Protects private identifiers in recent sessions: long digit runs are blurred again, including phone-number-like iMessage session IDs.
- Improves disconnected behavior: setup-oriented cards still show when Gateway data is not available, so first-run and broken-connection flows do not regress.
- Handles quiet or partial installs cleanly: empty sessions, missing cron jobs, api-key-only auth, absent quota data, and unavailable logs all get deliberate fallback states.

## Review fixes
- Restores digit-run blurring for connected Overview recent-session names and adds a regression fixture for a numeric iMessage-style direct-session identifier.
- Routes the new operator-dashboard user-facing copy through Control UI i18n keys and syncs shipped locale bundles/metadata.
- Replaces the Codex-specific usage card title with provider/model usage wording while still showing concrete provider names and provider-reported quota labels when providers supply usage data.
- Uses quota data from any provider that reports it, including API-key providers, while keeping the separate auth-health card focused on OAuth/token providers that can expire. Self-describing labels like OpenRouter `Credits` are no longer forced into `Credits quota`.
- Applies the same provider-native quota formatting to the chat header pill, so OpenRouter-style `Credits` renders with native remaining labels such as `$12.34 left` instead of `Credits quota` plus a normalized percentage.
- Adds provider-native quota display labels to the shared usage model, so balance providers can show dollar/credit amounts while percentage fields still support sorting and pressure ranking.
- Adds OpenRouter usage fetching for `/api/v1/credits` and `/api/v1/key`, displaying account credits/key limits as dollar balances when OpenRouter returns them.
- Tightens the first-screen layout by capping Attention/Recent Sessions rows and making the usage/quota card a compact metric grid.

## Screenshots
Redacted connected Overview screenshot from the current branch UI. This local Gateway capture currently returned no provider quota limits, so it shows the deliberate no-quota fallback state:

![Redacted connected OpenClaw Overview dashboard screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-pr-proof-redacted.png)

Redacted provider-quota fixture showing an OpenRouter-style dollar credits balance alongside a Codex 5h reset and a Z.ai token window. This fixture illustrates provider-shaped quota rendering without claiming the local install had live OpenRouter quota data at capture time:

![Redacted OpenRouter-style provider quota fixture screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-openrouter-quota-redacted.png)

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts extensions/openrouter/usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:sync`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `pnpm lint:ui:no-raw-window-open`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Connected Control UI Overview should show the compact operator dashboard while preserving privacy for recent-session identifiers, avoiding unbounded first-screen card height, and presenting model/provider usage without making OpenClaw look Codex-only or forcing credit/balance providers into percentage-only labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Local working OpenClaw Gateway at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/`, with the current branch Control UI bundle served locally for capture. The running installed Gateway was older than the branch, so provider usage was also verified directly through the current branch usage loader.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the repo, ran focused Overview/chat render tests plus i18n/build/diff checks, opened Overview with Playwright against the local Gateway, redacted private text in the DOM, and captured screenshots. A second redacted fixture patches the visible provider-usage panel to illustrate OpenRouter dollar-credit display alongside a Codex reset window and a Z.ai token window.
- **Evidence after fix:** The current branch provider-usage loader returned real Codex windows:

```json
{
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "displayName": "Codex",
  "windows": [
    { "label": "5h", "usedPercent": 10 },
    { "label": "Week", "usedPercent": 15 }
  ]
}
```

The refreshed connected Overview screenshot rendered the no-quota fallback when the local Gateway returned no provider limits:

```text
QUOTA
unavailable
Model auth loaded, but no provider quota limits were returned; showing local session usage.
```

The OpenRouter/Codex render fixture now shows dollar remaining labels and a Codex reset row without repeating that reset in a footer:

```text
CREDITS
12%
OpenRouter · Credits    $12.34 left
Codex · 5h              reset 2h 9m   15% left
Z.ai · Tokens (6h)      reset 2h 59m  60% left
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The connected Overview rendered the operator dashboard and the provider-quota fallback matched the local Gateway data. The OpenRouter/Codex fixture demonstrates provider-native dollar balances and Codex reset rows while retaining normalized percentages for ranking and for providers whose APIs report percentage/count windows. The redundant prose footer is suppressed when provider quota rows already carry the provider/limit/reset details.
- **What was not tested:** No live OpenRouter account balance was queried on the local install; the OpenRouter screenshot remains a labeled fixture. Broader cross-browser visual QA remains ordinary maintainer review scope.

## Implementation notes
- This PR intentionally avoids new Gateway RPCs. Message queue state, update posture, and host-health exceptions are represented only when existing dashboard data can support them.
- Follow-up telemetry could make the dashboard stronger by exposing pending sends/retries, installed-vs-latest version, restart-needed state, broader provider/quota signals, and host capacity alerts directly.

target.title: Preserve xAI usage limit errors in local TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- keep local TUI error handling from replacing known billing/rate-limit backend errors with the generic auth hint
- add xAI provider failover classification for Grok spending, credit, usage, quota, and rate-limit error codes
- cover the xAI classifier and TUI preservation behavior with focused tests

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xai/index.test.ts src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts`

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: local TUI runs now preserve backend text for billing/rate-limit failures, and xAI/Grok provider errors with known usage/spending codes classify into OpenClaw's standard `billing` or `rate_limit` lanes.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree with targeted Vitest coverage for xAI plugin registration, TUI chat error events, provider hook routing, and shared provider error patterns.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xai/index.test.ts src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: 5 test files passed, 193 tests passed; `git diff --check` passed; focused oxlint command exited cleanly.

Observed result after fix: xAI `SPENDING_LIMIT`-style errors classify as `billing`, xAI `USAGE_LIMIT`/`RATE_LIMIT_REACHED`-style errors classify as `rate_limit`, and the TUI renders the backend error instead of the `/auth` hint for a local xAI spending-limit failure.

What was not tested: live xAI account exhaustion was not exercised; the PR uses documented/plugin architecture plus representative backend error payloads.

target.title: Expose plugin approval action metadata
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: approval action metadata and no-route pending surface; it does not resolve verified approvals by itself. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? Summary: - Add plugin approval action templates to the before_tool_call approval result and plugin.approval.request schema. - Expand templates with the server-generated approval id, store the resulting action commands, and carry them through reply payloads and approval view models. - Add keepPendingWithoutRoute so external HITL plugins can keep plugin approvals pending when no core approval route/client is active. - Keep native decision-button channel adapters compatible with command-only approval actions. - Update the generated Swift protocol model and plugin SDK API baseline for the intentional additive surface change. Verification: - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: external plugins can attach approval-specific command actions to plugin approval requests and keep those requests pending while their own HITL command flow is responsible for resolution. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased onto current `origin/main` on May 16, 2026. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the protocol generator/check, plugin SDK API baseline check, extension typecheck, plugin SDK strict declaration smoke, targeted extension oxlint, focused approval/gateway/plugin Vitest coverage, the host hook contract test, and whitespace diff check. Evidence after fix: ```text $ pnpm protocol:check wrote dist/protocol.schema.json wrote apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm tsgo:extensions (exit 0) $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts Found 0 warnings and 0 errors. $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts Test Files 10 passed (10) Tests 122 passed (122) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 50 passed (50) $ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD (no output) ``` Observed result after fix: the protocol and SDK baselines reflect the new additive action metadata, representative adapters preserve command actions, approval view/reply rendering carries those commands, and the before_tool_call host hook contract remains compatible. What was not tested: full release suite, live World ID callback flow, and the follow-up verified plugin-owned resolution API that is split into #82434. Refs #82336 ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's approval actions and no-route pending behavior. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status ✓ main CI · 25972624778 ✓ build in 1m46s ✓ Run pnpm build gatewayWatch ✓ Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke ✓ Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source ✓ Run pnpm test:hitl ✓ Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, the plugin SDK declarations expose the needed approval/action surface, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82434 and #82471 for the full HITL path.

target.title: Doctor: expose UI freshness health findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps real build/rebuild mutation on the existing `maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness()` path used by `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Keeps existing doctor repairs in their positional slots: the pre-health UI freshness step still calls the legacy repair path, and the broad structured runner now excludes positional-owned health checks.
- Hardens the stale detector so a successful empty git history does not create false stale findings, while unavailable git history still reports the mtime mismatch without commit details.
- Skips build/rebuild prompts when the checkout does not contain `ui/` sources.

This PR does not change the public health/plugin SDK contract, does not invent file diffs for generated UI assets, and does not move existing doctor repair order into the broad structured runner.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw doctor --lint` can report missing/stale Control UI assets, and dry-run repair output can preview the build/rebuild process effect without mutating generated UI files or changing the existing `openclaw doctor --fix` repair ownership.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-ui-freshness-stack`, head `f2a6b4af48`, source harness using the real doctor health registry and temporary ignored `dist/control-ui/index.html` states. Paths below are redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git diff --check`
2. `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts`

4. `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`
5. `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
6. `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
7. `node --import tsx /tmp/84290-real-proof.mjs`
8. `bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
Focused validation passed: oxfmt reported all matched files use the correct format; oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors; Vitest reported 5 files and 47 tests passed; core and core-test tsgo exited 0; autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Redacted behavior proof output:

```text
--- missing assets lint JSON ---
exitCode=1
{"ok":false,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":55,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness","severity":"warning","message":"- Control UI assets are missing.\n- Run: pnpm ui:build","path":"/redacted/openclaw/dist/control-ui/index.html","fixHint":"Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to build Control UI assets."}]}
--- missing assets dry-run repair result ---
{
  "checksRun": 1,
  "checksRepaired": 1,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness",
```

Observed result after fix:
The structured health check reports missing Control UI assets and stale assets through lint JSON. Dry-run repair returns preview process effects for build/rebuild without generated file diffs. Real doctor --fix repair remains in the existing pre-health UI freshness slot, while the broad structured runner excludes positional-owned checks. When git can prove there are no schema commits since the UI build, no stale finding is emitted; when git history is unavailable, the mtime mismatch remains reportable without commit details. Slim/source-limited installs do not get prompted to run an impossible UI rebuild.

What was not tested:

target.title: fix(agents): wait for compaction before requester steering fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86566 (follow-up to #85489). Active requester steering treated a `compacting` outcome from `queueEmbeddedPiMessageWithOutcome` as a terminal wake failure, so completion delivery fell through to the requester-agent/direct fallback even though the active run becomes steerable again as soon as compaction finishes.

This introduces a shared `resolveActiveWakeWithRetries` helper used by **both** active-wake call sites — the steer path (`maybeSteerSubagentAnnounce`) and the generated-completion active wake (`sendSubagentAnnounceDirectly`). The helper treats `compacting` as transient: it waits through compaction and retries the same wake, bounded by cancellation and a finite backoff schedule that stays well within the announce delivery timeout. The best-effort transcript-commit retry and the compaction retry share a single loop, so a run that compacts and then reports `transcript_commit_wait_unsupported` still gets the best-effort retry. Other wake failures (`no_active_run`, `not_streaming`, delivery-mode mismatch, runtime rejection) keep their existing single-attempt fallback behavior.

Addresses the earlier review notes: the compaction retry now also covers the completion wake path, and the best-effort fallback is preserved after compaction by handling both transient retries in one loop.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: when an active requester is compacting and compaction finishes within the delivery timeout, both the steer path and the generated-completion wake should wait and re-steer the same active run instead of falling back to direct requester-agent delivery.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Exercised the real `maybeSteerSubagentAnnounce` and `sendSubagentAnnounceDirectly` paths through `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` under the repo's vitest runner, with no mocking of the module under test (only the queue-outcome dependency is driven to simulate the compacting-then-ready sequence). Compared the pre-fix and post-fix code.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the subagent announce delivery suite, including regression tests that drive the steer queue and the completion-handoff wake to return `compacting` first and a queued success second, plus a guard test that a non-compacting failure is not retried.

Evidence after fix: stdout from the vitest run against the real steering and completion paths, comparing pre-fix and post-fix:
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t 86566
-- BEFORE the fix (compacting is terminal on both paths): regression tests fail --
x waits through compaction and re-steers the active requester (86566)
AssertionError: expected 'direct' to deeply equal 'steered'
x waits through compaction on the completion handoff wake (86566)
AssertionError: expected 'direct' to deeply equal 'steered'

does not retry non-compacting steer failures (86566)
Tests  4 failed | 2 passed | 126 skipped (132)

-- AFTER the fix (compacting is transient on both paths): all pass --

waits through compaction and re-steers the active requester (86566)
waits through compaction on the completion handoff wake (86566)
does not retry non-compacting steer failures (86566)
Tests  6 passed | 126 skipped (132)


Observed result after fix: before the fix a compacting outcome on either the steer path or the generated-completion wake routes the completion to the direct fallback path (`path: "direct"`); after the fix each path waits through compaction and re-attempts the same wake, so the completion stays on the steering path (`path: "steered"`) and the queue is attempted twice. A non-compacting failure (`no_active_run`) is attempted only once and is not retried. The full subagent-announce-delivery suite (132 tests) passes.

What was not tested: no live multi-agent run with a real compaction cycle was exercised; the compacting-then-ready transition is simulated through the queue-outcome dependency on the real steering and completion code paths. The retry delays use the existing test-fast schedule so the bounded backoff is exercised without real waits.

target.title: feat(signal): support reaction approvals
target.body:
Summary

- Add Signal native exec/plugin approval capability gated by top-level approvals configuration.
- Add thumbs-up / thumbs-down reaction approval handling for allow-once and deny, with explicit Signal approver auth.
- Bind reactions only from native approval delivery, verify target-author identity, persist binding state, and re-check current approval route config before resolving.
- Suppress duplicate local exec approval fallback messages after native Signal approval delivery succeeds.

Verification

- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts

- [x] uvx showboat verify .mem/main/proofs/demo-29-prod-signal-single-exec-approval/raw/showboat-summary.md
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: real inbound `touch /tmp/foo` produced exactly one exec approval request and `/approve <id> allow-once` resolved it.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: synthetic exec approval prompt rendered `React with: 👍 Allow Once / 👎 Deny`; a real `signal-cli sendReaction --emoji 👍` against the exact bot-authored approval message resolved `allow-once`.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: with `channels.signal.allowFrom` temporarily empty, the approval prompt delivered without reaction choices and a 👍 reaction did not resolve within 6s.
- [x] Live prod Signal proof: with `channels.signal.allowFrom` restored, a 👍 reaction sent to a non-matching target timestamp did not resolve within 6s.

Behavior addressed: Signal can deliver native approval prompts and resolve authorized 👍 / 👎 reactions as allow-once / deny without cross-conversation timestamp fallback or duplicate local exec approval fallback messages.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout, prod OpenClaw profile, real Signal channel via sender-side `signal-cli` receive/sendReaction artifacts with private account data redacted.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod claw-debug send-approval --type exec signal --to <redacted Signal target>`; `signal-cli -a <redacted sender> receive`; `signal-cli -a <redacted sender> sendReaction --emoji 👍 --target-author <redacted bot> --target-timestamp <approval-message-timestamp> <redacted bot>`; repeated with empty `channels.signal.allowFrom` and with a wrong target timestamp; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.
Evidence after fix: authorized reaction proof returned `exec approval 634e7966-5721-40b8-abf6-72e03da4d318 approved with allow-once` and Signal received `✅ Exec approval allow-once. Resolved by Signal approval`; empty-allowFrom proof for `1c174127-f727-45af-a052-2664201c263e` stayed pending after 6s; wrong-target proof for `c1970b61-13ea-4a15-a93d-48e2b6b1387e` stayed pending after 6s; Showboat real-command proof passed with one post-send approval prompt and one final completion.
Observed result after fix: authorized exact-message Signal reactions resolve the intended approval, missing approvers do not get reaction choices and do not resolve by reaction, wrong-target reactions do not resolve the approval, and real Signal exec approval delivery no longer double-sends the local fallback prompt.
What was not tested: live plugin approval reaction roundtrip; plugin native rendering and reaction decision plumbing are covered by focused tests in this PR.

target.title: refactor: centralize inbound supplemental context
target.body:
## Summary

Moves more inbound context responsibility into core. Channel plugins now pass canonical supplemental/facts payloads; core owns visibility filtering, media payload projection, lazy quote-media merge, self-reply suppression, and legacy prompt-field projection.

What changed:

- `finalizeInboundContext` accepts `MsgContext.SupplementalContext` and projects it into existing `ReplyTo*`, `Forwarded*`, `Thread*`, `GroupSystemPrompt`, and untrusted-context fields before prompt normalization.
- `finalizeChannelInboundContext` centralizes supplemental filtering, media payload merge, optional async quote-media resolution, self-quote body/media suppression, injected finalizer support, and hidden quote/forward/thread diagnostics.
- `buildChannelInboundEventContext` routes through the core finalizer while preserving the public sync/async SDK contract: sync by default, `Promise` only for `resolveSupplementalMedia: true`.
- Bundled/internal channel callers moved to `buildChannelInboundEventContext` or `runtime.channel.turn.buildContext`; direct finalizer usage is now a deprecated public compatibility island.
- Discord and Telegram await `buildChannelInboundEventContext({ resolveSupplementalMedia: true })`; channel code still owns native fetch/parsing, core owns merge/suppression/finalization.
- Matrix, Feishu, iMessage, WhatsApp dispatch, QQBot, Slack, MS Teams, Signal, Google Chat, Synology Chat, Tlon, Twitch, Zalo, and Zalo User now use the builder/turn-builder path for inbound context payload construction.
- `resolveChannelInboundSupplementalContext` remains exported as a deprecated SDK wrapper for compatibility.
- Deprecated SDK type aliases remain exported so external plugin TypeScript imports keep compiling.
- Preserved existing legacy flat fields; supplemental projection fills missing values only.
- Added regression coverage for WhatsApp remote-only inbound media URLs.

API/deprecation notes:

- Deprecated: `filterChannelTurnSupplementalContext` alias.
- Deprecated: `resolveChannelInboundSupplementalContext`; prefer `buildChannelInboundEventContext({ resolveSupplementalMedia: true })` for inbound payloads.
- Deprecated: `finalizeChannelInboundContext` for new channel code; kept for public compatibility when callers already prepared legacy prompt fields.
- Preferred: `buildChannelInboundEventContext` / `runtime.channel.turn.buildContext` for channel inbound payloads.
- Not deprecated: legacy `ReplyTo*`, `Forwarded*`, and `Thread*` fields. They remain runtime/template compatibility fields and are now projected by core.

Diff/LOC notes:

- Current PR diff vs merge-base `origin/main`: +1575 / -784, net +791.
- Production/runtime-ish triple-dot diff, excluding tests/docs/changelog: +1088 / -553, net +535.
- Final local dirty slice added by the last migration commit: +404 / -240 overall; prod-ish +306 / -166, net +140.
- This is not a LOC win. The win is the ownership boundary: bundled channels stop owning prompt projection/finalization, while public SDK compatibility remains intact.

## Verification

Latest proof after rebase, SDK compatibility fix, and final builder migration:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-compat-1.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-compat-2.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.systemPrompt.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.body-for-agent.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-topic-threadid.test.ts extensions/zalouser/src/monitor.group-gating.test.ts`
  - passed: 12 files, 200 tests after final rebase on `origin/main` `9b9d8970b0`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - passed: 1 file, 46 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-topic-threadid.test.ts`
  - passed: 3 files, 22 tests
- `git diff --check`
  - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search --output /tmp/inbound-refactor-autoreview-async-builder-3.txt --json-output /tmp/inbound-refactor-autoreview-async-builder-3.json`
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch is correct

Earlier focused channel proof during this refactor:

- Core finalization + reply inbound tests passed: 2 files, 28 tests.
- Matrix/MS Teams focused tests passed: 2 files, 25 tests.
- WhatsApp inbound context/dispatch tests passed before the final regression addition: 2 files, 48 tests.
- Signal/iMessage focused tests passed: 3 files, 48 tests.
- Telegram focused shared-builder and media tests passed.
- Discord focused context/process-message tests passed: 2 files, 77 tests.
- Feishu targeted quote/thread tests passed; QQBot full outbound-dispatch tests passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Inbound quote/forward/thread supplemental context is filtered, media-merged, and projected consistently in core while preserving channel-specific parsing, fetches, routing metadata, SDK sync/async compatibility, and Discord/Telegram self-reply suppression behavior.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout with focused core, plugin SDK, QQBot, WhatsApp, and representative rich-channel Vitest suites plus core/extension test typecheck.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See `Verification` above; latest commands were run after the rebase, SDK compatibility restoration, and WhatsApp remote-only media regression fix.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed for core finalization, SDK exports, Discord, Telegram, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, Matrix, MS Teams, Feishu, QQBot, and Zalo User surfaces; local autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after fixing SDK compatibility and WhatsApp remote-media findings.

Observed result after fix: Channels pass canonical `SupplementalContext`/facts or builder facts into core; `finalizeInboundContext` projects legacy fields once; lazy reply media is resolved by the finalizer path only when visible and not self-suppressed; remote-only WhatsApp inbound media URLs remain present in finalized context.

What was not tested: Full repository test suite, full CI completion, and live channel roundtrips were not run for this refactor PR.

target.title: docs: add existing-solutions preflight guardrail
target.body:
## Summary
- add a repo-root AGENTS.md rule requiring a lightweight existing-solutions check before custom builds
- add the same reusable guardrail to default workspace AGENTS templates and the Kubernetes starter AGENTS config
- keep wording scoped to open-source projects, maintained libraries, existing OpenClaw plugins, or free platforms; custom builds remain allowed when existing options are unsuitable, too expensive, unmaintained, unsafe, non-compliant, or explicitly requested

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Agent instruction surfaces now include a lightweight existing-solutions preflight before custom build proposals. The rule says to prefer open-source projects, maintained libraries, existing OpenClaw plugins, or free platforms when adequate; custom builds are allowed only when existing options are unsuitable, too expensive, unmaintained, unsafe, non-compliant, or explicitly requested; paid-service recommendations require explicit spend approval.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw repository workspace on branch `nox-4929-existing-solutions-preflight`, commit `dfaa251df4`, using the checked-in docs/templates/scripts that seed or instruct real OpenClaw agents.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm docs:list`, inspected checked-in agent workspace template and Kubernetes starter AGENTS paths, patched the selected files, then searched those exact files for the guardrail text and ran focused formatting/docs checks.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ rg -n "Existing solutions preflight|Existing Solutions Preflight|Before proposing or building a custom system" AGENTS.md docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.dev.md docs/reference/AGENTS.default.md scripts/k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml
scripts/k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml:40:    Before proposing or building a custom system, feature, workflow, tool, integration, or automation, do a brief check for open-source projects, maintained libraries, existing OpenClaw plugins, or free platforms that already solve it well enough. Prefer those when adequate. Build custom only when existing options are unsuitable, too expensive, unmaintained, unsafe, non-compliant, or the user explicitly asks for custom. Avoid paid-service recommendations unless the user explicitly approves spend. Keep this lightweight: a preflight gate, not a broad research assignment.
```

Observed result after fix: The guardrail is present in repo-root instructions, default workspace instructions, manual/dev workspace templates, and the Kubernetes starter AGENTS config. `pnpm format:check ...`, `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs ...`, and `git diff --check ...` passed for the touched files.

What was not tested: No full `pnpm check`, `pnpm test`, or `pnpm build`; this is instruction/docs/YAML text only. Direct `pnpm exec prettier --check ...` was not available because `prettier` is not installed; the repo formatter command `pnpm format:check ...` passed.

## Verification
- `pnpm docs:list`

target.title: fix(security): guard plugin modelPatterns with compileSafeRegex
target.body:
## Summary

`resolveModelSupportMatchKind` in `src/plugins/providers.ts` compiles plugin manifest `modelPatterns` strings with raw `new RegExp(patternSource, "u")`. A malicious or careless pattern like `(a+)+$` causes catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when tested against non-matching model IDs.

The codebase already has `compileSafeRegex()` in `src/security/safe-regex.ts` that detects nested repetition and rejects unsafe patterns. This PR wires it into the model pattern matching path.

**Changes:**
- Import `compileSafeRegex` in `providers.ts`
- Replace `new RegExp(patternSource, "u")` with `compileSafeRegex(patternSource, "u")` -- unsafe patterns return `null` and are skipped (same behavior as the previous `catch { continue }` for invalid regex, but also covers ReDoS patterns that are syntactically valid)
- Add a test verifying a `(a+)+$` pattern against adversarial input completes in <50ms instead of hanging

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Plugin manifest `modelPatterns` entries with nested quantifiers (e.g., `(a+)+$`) cause catastrophic backtracking in `resolveModelSupportMatchKind`, freezing the provider resolution hot path. With 30 repeated characters the raw RegExp hangs for over 5 seconds; with longer input it hangs indefinitely.

**Real environment tested:** macOS, Node 22.19, openclaw at commit 88a85a1fcd on branch `fix/provider-model-pattern-redos`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1. Demonstrate the vulnerability with raw RegExp (before fix behavior):

```bash
timeout 5 node -e "
const regex = new RegExp('(a+)+\$', 'u');
const input = 'a'.repeat(30) + '!';
console.log('Testing raw RegExp against 30 a + ! ...');
const start = performance.now();
regex.test(input);
console.log('Done in', (performance.now() - start).toFixed(0), 'ms');
" || echo 'TIMED OUT after 5 seconds (catastrophic backtracking)'
```

Step 2. Demonstrate the guard rejects the pattern and valid patterns still work:

```bash
node --import tsx -e "
import { compileSafeRegex } from './src/security/safe-regex.js';
const malicious = compileSafeRegex('(a+)+\$', 'u');
console.log('compileSafeRegex(\"(a+)+\$\"):', malicious);
const valid = compileSafeRegex('^qwen3\\\\.6-27b@iq3_xxs\$', 'u');
console.log('compileSafeRegex(\"^qwen3.6-27b@iq3_xxs\$\"):', valid);
console.log('valid.test(\"qwen3.6-27b@iq3_xxs\"):', valid?.test('qwen3.6-27b@iq3_xxs'));
const input = 'a'.repeat(30) + '!';
const start = performance.now();
const result = malicious?.test(input) ?? 'pattern rejected';
console.log('Adversarial input:', result, 'in', (performance.now() - start).toFixed(2), 'ms');
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before (raw RegExp, unpatched behavior):
```
$ timeout 5 node -e "const regex = new RegExp('(a+)+$', 'u'); ..."
Testing raw RegExp against 30 a + ! ...
TIMED OUT after 5 seconds (catastrophic backtracking)
```

After (compileSafeRegex guard, patched behavior):
```
$ node --import tsx -e "import { compileSafeRegex } from './src/security/safe-regex.js'; ..."
compileSafeRegex("(a+)+$"): null
compileSafeRegex("^qwen3\.6-27b@iq3_xxs$"): /^qwen3\.6-27b@iq3_xxs$/u
valid.test("qwen3.6-27b@iq3_xxs"): true
Adversarial input: pattern rejected in 0.00 ms
```

The malicious pattern is rejected (returns `null`) so the adversarial input never reaches the regex engine. Valid patterns like the LM Studio quant suffix pattern compile and match correctly.

**Observed result after fix:** The `compileSafeRegex` guard detects nested repetition in `(a+)+$` and returns `null`, preventing the pattern from being used. The adversarial input that causes a 5+ second hang with raw RegExp resolves in 0.00ms. Legitimate plugin patterns like `^qwen3\.6-27b@iq3_xxs$` compile and match correctly, confirming no regression in valid model pattern support.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway startup with a real installed plugin containing a malicious `modelPatterns` entry (would require crafting and installing a plugin package). The proof exercises the same `compileSafeRegex` guard function used by the patched code path.

target.title: fix(security): escape field names in transcript regex extraction
target.body:
## Summary

`extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` and `extractJsonNullableStringFieldPrefix` in `session-utils.fs.ts` interpolate the `field` parameter directly into `new RegExp(...)` without escaping. All current callers pass hardcoded field names (`"id"`, `"parentId"`, `"type"`, `"role"`), but the function signature accepts any `string`. A future caller passing a field containing regex metacharacters (e.g. `"foo.bar"`) would match unintended patterns (`.` matches any character instead of a literal dot).

This wraps the interpolation with `escapeRegExp()` from `src/shared/regexp.ts` so metacharacters are treated literally.

## Verification

219 session-utils tests pass. The `oversized line metadata extraction preserves id and parentId` test exercises the escapeRegExp-guarded regex path through the real `readRecentSessionMessagesAsync` public API with a 300KB oversized transcript line.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Regex injection via unescaped field name interpolation in oversized transcript metadata extraction (`session-utils.fs.ts:276,293`). The `extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` and `extractJsonNullableStringFieldPrefix` functions build `new RegExp()` from the `field` parameter. Without `escapeRegExp()`, metacharacter field names cause extraction failure or wrong-value extraction.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v24.15.0, arm64, openclaw dev checkout at commit 7876d85d59.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "oversized"` to run all 5 oversized transcript tests including the tightened metadata assertion. (2) Standalone `node` script exercising the before/after extraction functions with metacharacter field names.

Evidence after fix: All 5 oversized transcript tests pass. The tightened test directly asserts `__openclaw.id === "oversized-child"` (exact field match, not string containment) and verifies the record appears in active-tree output (proving parentId extraction worked). The standalone script below shows the before/after difference for metacharacter field names.

```console
$ pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "oversized"
 ✓ oversized transcript line guards > readRecentSessionMessagesAsync replaces oversized JSONL lines with placeholders
 ✓ oversized transcript line guards > readRecentSessionMessagesAsync keeps oversized active-tree leaves
 ✓ oversized transcript line guards > readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscriptAsync skips oversized lines
 ✓ oversized transcript line guards > oversized line metadata extraction preserves id and parentId
 ✓ oversized transcript line guards > readSessionTitleFieldsFromTranscriptAsync delegates to bounded sync reader

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed | 68 skipped (73)
```

The tightened test exercises the REAL `readRecentSessionMessagesAsync` → `parseRecentTranscriptTailMessages` → `parseTailTranscriptRecord` → `buildOversizedTranscriptRecord` → `extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` (with `escapeRegExp`) code path. It writes a 300KB oversized transcript line to disk and reads it back through the public API. The direct field assertions prove:
- `meta.id === "oversized-child"` (regex extraction produced the exact value)
- `out.length === 2` (parentId extraction worked: the tree selected both root and child)
- `serialized.not.toContain(oversizedContent)` (300KB content was replaced with placeholder)

Standalone metacharacter proof:
```console
$ node -e "
function escapeRegExp(s) { return s.replace(/[.*+?^\${}()|[\]\\\\]/g, '\\\\$&'); }
function extractBefore(prefix, field) {
  return new RegExp('\"' + field + '\"\\\\s*:\\\\s*\"((?:\\\\\\\\.|[^\"\\\\\\\\])*)\"').exec(prefix)?.[1];
}
function extractAfter(prefix, field) {
  return new RegExp('\"' + escapeRegExp(field) + '\"\\\\s*:\\\\s*\"((?:\\\\\\\\.|[^\"\\\\\\\\])*)\"').exec(prefix)?.[1];
}
const prefix = '{\"foo.bar\": \"correct\", \"fooXbar\": \"wrong\"}';
console.log('Before (no escape):', extractBefore(prefix, 'foo.bar'));
console.log('After  (escaped):', extractAfter(prefix, 'foo.bar'));
"
Before (no escape): wrong
After  (escaped): correct
```

Without `escapeRegExp`, `foo.bar` matches `fooXbar` (the `.` is a regex wildcard). With `escapeRegExp`, the `.` is escaped to `\.` and matches only the literal `foo.bar` field.

Observed result after fix: All 5 oversized tests pass with direct field assertions. The `escapeRegExp` wrapper in `extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` and `extractJsonNullableStringFieldPrefix` prevents regex metacharacter injection. Current callers use safe field names (`id`, `parentId`, `type`, `role`) so there is no behavioral change in production; the fix is defensive for future callers.

What was not tested: Live gateway session with a metacharacter field name (no production transcript contains such fields today). The standalone script and unit tests exercise the patched extraction logic directly.

target.title: fix(logging): exit on stdout/stderr EPIPE instead of spinning
target.body:
## Summary

- When the gateway process is orphaned after a systemd service restart, the parent's journal pipe closes and every write to stdout/stderr returns `EPIPE`
- The previous stream error handler swallowed it with a bare `return`, so background loops (chokidar config file watcher, etc.) kept firing and the process spun at ~100% CPU indefinitely — confirmed via `strace`: 6,000 `faccessat` calls/sec + failing `write` to fd 2 in a tight loop
- Exit cleanly with code 0 instead — a process whose own output streams are broken has nowhere to log and no reason to keep running

## Changes

- `src/logging/console.ts`: stream error handler now calls `process.exit(0)` on EPIPE/EIO for stdout/stderr instead of swallowing
- `src/logging/console-capture.test.ts`: updated test from `"swallows async EPIPE"` → `"exits on async EPIPE"`, mocks `process.exit` and asserts it is called with `0`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: After `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway`, the previous worker process is orphaned — its stdout/stderr pipe to journald closes. Every log attempt returned EPIPE, which was swallowed, so the chokidar config-file watcher kept looping at ~100% CPU indefinitely. The process ignored SIGTERM and had to be killed with `kill -9`.

**Real environment tested**: Raspberry Pi 5 (arm64, Raspberry Pi OS Bookworm), Node 22, `openclaw-gateway.service` systemd user service, gateway built from this branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Applied fix, rebuilt with `pnpm build`
2. `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway`
3. `pgrep -a node | grep 'dist/index.js gateway'`

**Evidence after fix**:

Before fix — strace on orphaned process PID 2505087 showed tight EPIPE loop:

```
$ sudo strace -p 2505087 -e trace=faccessat,write
faccessat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/pavel/.openclaw", F_OK) = 0
faccessat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/pavel/.openclaw/openclaw.json", F_OK) = 0
write(2, "...", 64)                     = -1 EPIPE (Broken pipe)
--- SIGPIPE {si_signo=SIGPIPE, si_code=SI_USER} ---
faccessat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/pavel/.openclaw", F_OK) = 0
faccessat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/pavel/.openclaw/openclaw.json", F_OK) = 0
write(2, "...", 64)                     = -1 EPIPE (Broken pipe)
--- SIGPIPE {si_signo=SIGPIPE, si_code=SI_USER} ---
[repeating ~6000 syscalls/sec, process at 99.8% CPU]
```

After fix — only the new worker remains after restart:

```
$ pgrep -a node | grep 'dist/index.js gateway'
3142891 node /home/pavel/openclaw/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
```

**Observed result after fix**: Single gateway process after service restart. No orphaned process accumulates CPU. `top` shows stable <5% CPU at idle. No need for `kill -9`.

**What was not tested**: Windows and macOS — both use stdout/stderr differently (no systemd journal pipe). The EPIPE scenario is Linux-specific but `process.exit(0)` on a broken output stream is safe on all platforms.

target.title: AI-assisted: fix(chat): bound chat history display payloads
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes `chat.history` display payload growth by adding bounded display projections before history responses are returned to callers.

It covers text, tool payloads, image/media blocks, canvas media references, Swift protocol surfaces, UI/controller handling, gateway tests, public protocol docs, and a beta2 fix report.

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR publication using OpenAI Codex `gpt-5.5` in the OpenClaw workspace. Operator reviewed the findings, evidence packet, and remediation plan before this update.

## Problem

`chat.history` could return oversized visible history payloads when transcripts contained large tool results, media blocks, or long message content. That created an availability risk for WebChat and gateway callers because history reads could become large enough to stall UI/session flows.

A late review pass also found that normal `messages` mode was capped to 64 KiB after safe tool redaction, which could unnecessarily drop safe text even when the caller configured a larger history budget.

## Root Cause

History projection and redaction were not consistently separated from raw transcript preservation. The display path needed bounded, caller-safe projections while keeping raw transcript data out of normal UI responses.

The final `messages` mode budget also applied a hard 64 KiB cap after redaction, rather than honoring the configured history budget for safe text.

## Solution

- Adds bounded chat display projection helpers for text, tool payloads, media, and canvas blocks.
- Routes `chat.history` display responses through bounded projections instead of exposing unbounded raw payload shape.
- Adds `mode: "messages" | "turns" | "raw-messages"` and gates raw payload inspection behind `operator.admin`.
- Preserves safe `messages` mode text up to the configured `maxHistoryBytes` budget after tool payload omission.
- Regenerates Swift protocol models from the canonical schema output.
- Documents the new history modes and `unsafeRawToolPayloads` admin guard in public WebChat/Gateway protocol docs.
- Adds gateway regression tests for bounded tool payloads, visible media shaping, recent visible turn preservation, placeholder semantics, and large safe text preservation.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `chat.history` was returning oversized display payloads (production logs showed >=88 KB responses and `readMs=1715-1893` even for tiny sessions, plus an 11050 ms WebSocket response), and could leak raw tool-call arguments / tool-result payloads into display history. This PR bounds display payloads, collapses tool turns by default, gates raw payloads behind `operator.admin`, and adds `mode=messages|turns|raw-messages` with omitted-mode defaulting to `turns` for display clients.

**Real environment tested:** Isolated child Gateway booted from the PR source tree at `f626813d8a8b5f3faa38a0674099906cef8ca454` on `forge/chat-history-display-bounds-v2`. Host `openclaw-prod` local shell, Node `v22.22.2`, ephemeral test token. Production Gateway not touched. Channels, providers, cron, and bundled plugins disabled in the child process. Transcript fixture is synthetic and redacted: 108 lines, 1,084,592 bytes, with embedded large tool results, assistant tool-call arguments, a canvas reference, and a media-path reference; marker strings used to detect raw-payload leakage. The child gateway ran with `gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback=true` and `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth=true`; those are test-harness break-glass flags only for 127.0.0.1 loopback, not changed runtime defaults under exercise.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```bash
PATH=/tmp/openclaw-proof-bin:$PATH \
  ./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/openclaw-chat-history-proof-86458.mts \
  > /tmp/openclaw-chat-history-proof-86458-after-f626813d.json
```
The harness boots an isolated Gateway, opens real WebSocket RPC clients with distinct client IDs, and calls `chat.history` against the oversized fixture for each surface: WebChat, TUI, macOS, iOS, Android, generic UI, CLI, plus explicit `mode=messages`, non-admin `raw-messages` (must reject), and admin `raw-messages` (must succeed).

**Evidence after fix:** Raw JSON `/tmp/openclaw-chat-history-proof-86458-after-f626813d.json` (190 lines, includes per-client results and Gateway stderr/stdout tail with the `chat.history metrics` lines from the actual run). Representative metric line copied from the isolated PR Gateway log:
```text
chat.history metrics mode=turns responseMode=turns rawToolPayloads=false sessionHash=0d6e4079e367 requestedLimit=50 max=50 scanMax=1000 maxChars=4000 maxHistoryBytes=6291456 finalHistoryBudgetBytes=6291456 localMessages=108 rawMessages=108 projectedMessages=108 normalizedMessages=27 returnedMessages=27 returnedItems=9 rawMessagesMatched=108 toolRecordsCollapsed=45 hasMoreBefore=false placeholders=0 safeToolPlaceholders=0 safeToolOmittedBytes=0 messagesBytesReturned=6438 responseBytesReturned=13092 sessionKnown=true transcriptRead=true readMs=12 readFileBytes=1084592 readBytes=1084592 readLines=108 readMode=full-tail totalMs=25
```
Production before-evidence from the same affected surface, redacted to session hashes, from `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-24.log`:
```text
2026-05-24T23:13:35.952Z chat.history metrics mode=messages sessionHash=ac38af406fed ... messagesBytesReturned=87915 responseBytesReturned=88104 readMs=1838 totalMs=1840
2026-05-24T23:13:35.947Z chat.history metrics mode=messages sessionHash=75794a8e1d45 ... messagesBytesReturned=57617 responseBytesReturned=57810 readMs=1890 totalMs=1893
2026-05-24T23:01:46.498Z chat.history metrics mode=messages sessionHash=a6923f4da653 ... localMessages=0 messagesBytesReturned=2 responseBytesReturned=128 readMs=1715 totalMs=1716
2026-05-24T06:19:28.690Z WebSocket chat.history response took 11050ms
```

**Observed result after fix:** On the PR build, omitted-mode display clients (WebChat, TUI, macOS, iOS, Android, generic UI) returned `mode=turns`, 27 normalized messages collapsed to 9 display items, 13,092 response bytes, no raw tool markers, no raw assistant tool-argument markers, safe visible text and canvas reference preserved, 45 tool records collapsed, `totalMs` 10-25 ms across repeated calls on the 1,084,592-byte transcript. Explicit `mode=messages` returned 50 messages, 12,118 bytes, no raw markers, `[chat.history tool payload omitted]` placeholders present. Non-admin `mode=raw-messages` rejected with Gateway code `INVALID_REQUEST` and message `chat.history raw-messages requires operator.admin scope`. Admin `mode=raw-messages` returned 50 messages and 494,324 bytes with raw markers present. CLI omitted-mode intentionally still defaults to `mode=messages` to preserve gateway-callable legacy semantics.

**Not tested:**
- Control UI / WebChat browser surface was not driven through a browser or screen-recording session in this proof. Compatibility was exercised at the Gateway WebSocket protocol layer with the WebChat client ID, not via DOM render.
- Same-fixture live run against the merge-base build was attempted, but the base worktree's `pnpm install` aborted non-interactively against a symlinked `node_modules`. Before evidence is therefore real production log lines from the affected surface rather than a same-fixture old-build replay.
- Codex final-2 P2 finding `meta.hasMoreBefore` saturated-window case is not exercised here: fixture `localMessages=108` vs `scanMax=1000`, so the window never saturates. This is deferred to a follow-up because addressing it now would mix a separate pagination bug into this display-payload bounding change.

## Validation

| Command / Gate | Result | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| Focused chat history regression suite | PASS | Earlier closeout run: 4 files, 142 tests |
| `corepack pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` | PASS | Fresh post-review run: 2 files, 132 tests |
| `CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed` | PASS | Fresh post-review run; includes local deadcode/dependency gate and notes SwiftLint is macOS-CI owned |
| Swift protocol generation | PASS | `GatewayModels.swift` regenerated to canonical output |
| Real behavior proof parser shape | PASS EXPECTED | This PR body now uses the required `## Real behavior proof` field labels and embeds non-mock command/log evidence |

## Review Findings Addressed

- ClawSweeper real-behavior-proof block: replaced prose proof with parser-shaped live evidence from an isolated Gateway run.
- ClawSweeper P2 docs finding: documented `chat.history` modes and `unsafeRawToolPayloads` guard in `docs/web/webchat.md` and `docs/gateway/protocol.md`.
- Bundled Swift protocol check: regenerated `GatewayModels.swift` instead of hand-editing optional defaults.
- Agent-chat tests: updated the gateway-callable helper expectations so placeholder redaction does not break semantic assertions.
- Dependency/deadcode check: fresh `check:changed` passes locally; the prior `ui/src/ui/control-ui-chunking.ts` orphan failure appears stale relative to this head.
- Copilot hot-path nits: avoided a second `augmentChatHistoryWithCanvasBlocks()` pass for non-raw history responses and cached `extractToolCallIds()` in turn aggregation.

## Remaining Follow-Up

- `meta.hasMoreBefore` can under-report when the scan window saturates. This is separate from the payload-bounding behavior in this PR and should be fixed in a focused pagination/windowing follow-up.
- The real proof did not include a browser screenshot or recording. WebSocket-level client compatibility is covered here; UI visual proof can be added if maintainers want browser evidence.

## Risk Before Merge

- Compatibility: `chat.history` response shape is intentionally bounded for display modes. Raw/debug access remains behind the explicit admin raw path.
- Message delivery / UI availability: this reduces large-history pressure, but maintainers should still watch WebChat history latency on beta2.
- Session-state: history display output changes should be checked against existing UI expectations and Swift clients.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` entry was added. OpenClaw contributor guidance leaves changelog handling to maintainers or landing automation.
